preamble: A complex shift where a labial stop [b] transforms into a voiceless alveolar affricate [ts], usually through palatalization and devoicing stages.

examples.note: Intermediate stages in labial-to-dental consonant developments.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
